back_translation: Then Jesus spoke a parable to them, in order to teach that they should pray continually and not lose heart.
He said, "In a certain city, there was a judge who did not fear God and did not regard people at all.
In that city there was a widow; she kept coming to him repeatedly, pleading, 'Please grant me justice against my legal adversary.'
He was unwilling to comply for a while, but afterward he thought in his heart, 'Though I do not fear God and do not regard people,
yet because this widow troubles me, I will give her justice, so that she will not keep coming to bother me and wear me out.'"
Then the Lord said, "Listen to what the unjust judge said.
Will God then not give justice to those he has chosen, who cry out to him day and night? Will he delay toward them?
I tell you that he will give justice to them quickly. Nevertheless, when the Son of Man comes, will he find faith on earth?"
He also spoke this parable to some who were confident in themselves that they were righteous and who despised others:
"Two men went up to the temple to pray; one was a Pharisee and the other was a tax collector.
That Pharisee stood up and prayed within himself, 'O God, I thank you that I am not like other people — cheats, unjust ones, adulterers, or even like this tax collector.
I fast two days per week; I give a tithe of everything that I earn.'
But the tax collector stood at a distance, did not dare even to lift his face to look up to heaven, but beat his chest and said, 'O God, please have mercy on me, the sinner.'

I tell you that this man returned to his home with God having cleansed him to be righteous, not that Pharisee. For everyone who exalts himself will be made low, but the one who humbles himself will be raised up."
The people were also bringing their infants to him, so that he might touch those children; but when the disciples saw this, they rebuked them.
But Jesus called those children and said, "Permit the little children to come to me; do not forbid them, because the kingdom of God belongs to such people as these.
I tell you the truth: whoever does not receive the kingdom of God like a little child will never enter it at all."
A certain nobleman asked him, saying, "Good teacher, what must I do to receive eternal life as an inheritance?"
Jesus said to him, "Why do you call me good? No one is good at all, except God alone.
You already know the commandments: 'Do not commit adultery, do not kill, do not steal, do not bear false witness, honor your father and mother.'"
But he said, "All these I have kept since my youth."
When Jesus heard this, he said to him, "You still lack one thing. Go sell everything you have and distribute to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven. After that, come follow me."
But when he heard this, he became extremely sad, because he was a very wealthy man.
Jesus looked at him and said, "How very hard it is for those who have wealth to enter the kingdom of God!
For it is still easier for a camel to pass through the eye of a needle than for a wealthy man to enter the kingdom of God."
Those who heard then asked, "If that is so, who can be saved?"
He said, "The things that are impossible for people are possible for God."
Peter said, "Look, we have left the things that are our own and have followed you."
He said to them, "I tell you the truth: there is no one who has left home, wife, brothers, parents, or children for the sake of the kingdom of God,
who will not receive many times more in this age, and in the age that is coming will receive eternal life."
Jesus took the twelve disciples aside and said to them, "Behold, we are going up to Jerusalem, and everything that the prophets have written concerning the Son of Man will be fulfilled.
For he will be handed over to the Gentiles; he will be mocked, insulted, and spat upon,
and they will flog him and then kill him, and on the third day he will rise again."
But they did not understand any of these things; this word was hidden from them, and they did not know the meaning of what he said.
When Jesus was drawing near to the city of Jericho, a certain blind man was sitting and begging beside the road.
When he heard the sound of a crowd passing by, he asked what was happening.
They told him, "Jesus the Nazarene is passing by."
So he cried out, "Jesus, Son of David, please have mercy on me!"
Those walking ahead forbade him to be silent, but he cried out with an even louder voice, "Son of David, please have mercy on me!"
Jesus stopped and commanded that the man be brought to him. When he came near, he asked him,
"What do you want me to do for you?" The man said, "Lord, please let me see again."
Jesus said to him, "See! Your faith has saved you."
Immediately he regained his sight and followed him, praising God. And all the people who saw this event also joined in praising God.
